lines.text: English speaking course.
Your failure isn't her fault.
You got into fashion school months ago, Ellie.
Wait, what's happening here? What led them to argue like that? Maybe we should go back to the beginning. That's a good
idea.
Your failure isn't her fault.
Guys, I got in.
I got into Rochester University engineering.
I knew it. I knew you could do it, Lucy.
Can you believe it? I'll be in those modern labs learning from the best professors.
Wow, great, Lucy. I'm really happy for you.
Jenny, you okay? Why do you sound Oh, don't worry about me.
I didn't get accepted. I found out last night, actually.
So, I guess my dream is over.
Don't say that, Jenny. And of course I'm worried about you.
You're my best friend.
Best friends don't leave each other behind.
We promised to go to New York together.
Maybe if I hadn't spent so much time helping you study.
What are you talking about, Jenny?
You chose to help her. That was your decision.
Your failure isn't her fault.
You got into fashion school months ago, Ellie. You don't know how this feels.
I'm so sorry you didn't get in, Jenny.
I didn't know you felt this way. Maybe I shouldn't have You don't have to apologize to her,
Lucy.
You studied hard for this.
Oh, right. You don't have to be sorry, Lucy.
Just go enjoy your perfect dream.
She is really hurt, Ellie.
She said I'm leaving her behind. I broke our promise.
Listen to me, Lucy.
A good friend helps you move forward, not try to stop you.
But what Jenny is doing isn't supporting you. She wants you to fail with her.
She's been my friend for so long.
I know, and everyone in your life is good for you.
Sometimes the best choice is to walk away from a relationship that holds you back.
You are right, Ellie. Thank you.
We knew you'd make it.
Dude, you've been staring at that coffee for 10 minutes. What's up?
It's my review meeting today with Ms. Davis. It's in 15 minutes.
Oh, that. Relax. Everyone has to go through it.
You'll be fine.
How? What if she asks about the Jenkins file?
The one I sent wrong last month?
Okay, rule number one, just be honest.
Say you made a mistake. No excuses.
Yes, I apologize for that. There was a mix-up and I sent the wrong report.
And don't stop there. Tell her what you learned from it.
Like?
After that, I always double-check my work with the team before I send it.
It shows you're serious about your job.
Be honest, then talk about the lesson.
One more thing, don't sound like you're asking to stay.
Then what should I sound like?
Like someone who already belongs here.
Say you see a future here and you want to help the company grow.
Okay, I can do this. Thanks, guys.
So, Alex, how do you feel about your first 3 months here?
It's been good. Of course, there were some challenges, but I learned from each one.
You meant the client report?
Yes, that one.
The good thing was the team helped me fix it right away.
We even created a quick checklist so it won't happen again.
That's good to hear.
And looking ahead, where do you see yourself?
Well, I really enjoy being here.
I like the team and the work we do.
I'd love to stay and help the company grow.
Mhm. I'm happy to tell you the review went well.
You passed, Alex.
Hey, guys. I passed.
Woah.
Welcome to the team, officially. We knew you'd make it.
>> [music] >> Are you guys together now?
So, don't make me wait.
How was the date with Dr. Dreamy Martin?
It was perfect.
Seriously.
Details. I need details.
We met in the morning and got coffee.
Then we went for a walk in the park.
Aw.
We were just talking and he actually listened.
Oh, Allison.
Later on, we went to see a movie. That new romantic comedy everyone's talking about.
And?
And for dinner, he took me to this Italian place with candles on every table.
It was so romantic.
He was really nice and funny.
This is it. He's the one, right?
Are you guys together now? When's the next date?
No, it's not going to happen.
Why not?
You said it was perfect.
Because they are not okay with it.
They? Who's they?
You mean his parents? Are they Not his parents.
Then who? His friends? His pet?
Tell me. I'll fight them.
His wife and his 4-year-old son.

He was my savior.
I still can't believe your parents let you have a dog.
Weren't they super against pet hair in the house?
Oh, they were. No pets, too much mess.
But Buddy here, he was my savior.
Your savior? How?
When I was seven, I walked through this park every day.
And I'd see him here all skinny.
I started giving him some of my food.
Your parents found out, right?
Yeah, they were very mad, so I stopped going to the park for a while.
So, what changed?
My parents took me to a festival there.
It was crowded and I got lost.
Then a man came up to me [music] and asked me to go with him.
Yo, dude.
Out of nowhere Buddy appeared. He started barking loudly at the man.
The man got scared and quickly ran away.
Not all heroes wear capes, huh, Buddy?
And he stayed right by my side until my parents found us.
And after that, Buddy became family?
Yep. Now my parents always call him David's bodyguard.
It's just a bad cold.
My dear sister, if I don't make it, tell Mom and Dad I love them.
You have a cold, Mark. Stop being such a drama queen.
It's not a cold. It's the plague. Aren't you in medical school?
Do something.
Fine. Tell me what's wrong.
But talk like a normal patient.
My head, it's boom boom boom.
Your head is pounding. That's a headache. What else?
My nose is like a waterfall.
So, a runny nose. Noted.
And my eyes, they're all wet and sad.
Watery eyes, normal with a cold.
Anything else? Cough? sore throat?
No. Headache, runny nose, watery eyes.
That's a full body attack.
Again, it's just a bad cold.
All you need is soup, lots of water, and less complaining.
Worst doctor ever.
All right. I'll have my chicken soup by myself.
I mean, best sister ever. And one bowl of soup, no carrots, please.
Was anyone hurt?
Daniel, sweetie.
Mom? What's wrong? Something happened?
I just saw the news. There was a building on fire on your street. Are you all right?
Mom, calm down. I live in the building across the street. I'm safe here.
Oh, thank goodness. What happened?
They think the fire started from a gas leak in the apartment on the 15th floor.
A gas leak? That's so dangerous.
The fire was huge.
And I saw firefighters pull people to safety.
It took them nearly half an hour to put out the fire.
Oh dear. Was anyone hurt?
Two people were seriously hurt and four more were lightly burned.
Oh, those poor people.
Daniel, please promise me you'll be extra careful.
I promise, Mom. I will.
Remember to check the stove before you leave. And Mom, it's okay. My building has good smoke alarms and everything.
I know, but I'm your mother. Worrying is what I do.
Call me again tomorrow, all right?
I will. Love you, Mom.
>> [music]
>> Moving in.
>> [music]
>> Why me?
Why always me? I am not Cinderella.
Lilly, it's just your turn to clean the house.
Yeah, check the chore chart. Your name's there.
I checked. My name is there three times this month. Three.
That's because you skipped last month.
>> [laughter] >> And the month before.
Are you guys calling me lazy?
>> [music]
>> Uh hi. I'm Sarah, your new housemate.
Oh, Sarah. I'm Jake. Thank goodness you're here. I mean, welcome.
Tom here. Good to see fresh energy.
Am I interrupting? Should I come back later?
No, no, no. Please stay.
Hi, Sarah. I am Lilly. I am the victim here. Nice to meet you.
Hi, Lilly. Victim of what?
Cleaning. They expect me to move the sofa.
She cries every time it's her cleaning day.
Um I can help with cleaning, no problem.
Let me go unpack first.
Really? You're already my favorite housemate.
Welcome to our crazy house,
Party planning.
So for Sarah's welcome party, I'm thinking we're going big. Like balloons, lights.
Our budget is like $50 total.
You guys really don't have to throw me a party. I'm just happy to be here.
Don't have to? Sarah, welcoming a new housemate is a tradition.
She's not wrong. We did it for everyone.
Though, yours might be less messy.
Remember Jake's party? Lily tried to make a chocolate fountain with a desk lamp.
That was creative. This time, what about karaoke?
I do love karaoke.
What about food?
Pizza. Easy, and everyone likes it.
For a welcome party?
How about I cook something? I can do some chicken wings, macaroni and cheese, and desserts.
Yes, home cooking is way better than pizza.
So, karaoke, Sarah's food, some decorations.
This will be the best welcome party ever.
Thanks, guys. This really means a lot to me.
Breakfast together.
>> [music]
>> Morning, Lily. I'm making breakfast for everyone. Coffee?
You're making breakfast?
For all of us?
What time did you wake up?
Around 6. I'm used to getting up early.
Want to help?
Sure, I'll help.
Great. Can you help me with the pancakes?
Uh okay.
But I usually burn things.
Don't worry, I'll help you. You only need to pour the batter into the pan.
When do I flip it?
See those bubbles? Now you can flip it.
Hey, it's not burned.
See? You're doing great.
>> [music] >> Wow, smells so good in here. Did someone order breakfast?
Nope, Lily and I made it together. She was a great helper.
Lily cooked without setting anything on fire?
These look amazing.
This is actually fun. Can we do this every weekend?
Of course.
First day at work.
>> [music] >> Welcome to Morrison Marketing, Sarah.
I'm Carlos Rodriguez, your manager.
Nice to meet you, Mr. Rodriguez. Thank you for this opportunity.
Please, call me Carlos. Let me show you around and introduce you to the team.
This is our main workspace. Everyone here works on different projects.
Oh, you must be the new hire. I'm Emma, graphic designer.
Hi Emma, I'm Sarah, content strategist.
Nice to meet you.
Welcome aboard. I'm David, I handle social media.
That's my area, too. Looking forward to working together.
Where did you work before, Sarah?
I was in Chicago, but I got transferred here to Boston. New city, new everything.
Wow, that's a big change.
Okay, now that you've met everyone, let me show you your desk and get you started.
If you need anything, just ask. We're here to help.
Thanks, everyone. I feel like I'm in good hands here.
Great job, everyone. You're doing very well so far.
You're one [music] step closer to your goal. Keep practicing and remember, you can do this.
Lunch with co-workers.
Hey Sarah, want to join us for lunch?
There's a great spot down the street.
Come on.
Oh, sure. Coming.
I'll take the salmon salad.
So, Sarah, how's week one treating you?
Honestly, better than I thought.
Nobody's yelled at me yet, so that's a win.
>> [laughter] >> Are you settling in okay?
Moving from Chicago to Boston can't be easy.
It's different, but my housemates have rescued me daily.
That's nice. It's important to have good people around you when you're adjusting.
What about food? Found any good places yet?
Not really. I've been cooking at home mostly.
Well then, you have to try the chicken sandwich or pasta salad here. They're amazing.
Mhm, the chicken sandwich sounds great.
And there's a great taco place two blocks away. We should go there sometime.
Ha, I think you're secretly working as a food tour guide.
Only on weekends. Weekdays, I just pretend to do social media.
Careful, Sarah. Once you follow David, your kitchen will get lonely forever.
>> [music] >> Grocery shopping.
Sarah, look at these fancy apples.
Those cost $4 each, Lily.
But Nah-uh, we need to stick to our budget.
Okay, responsible queen. You take all the fun out of shopping.
I'll take that as a compliment.
Now, we already have bread, milk, eggs.
Sarah, they have chocolate ice cream.
Can we get it, please?
Ice cream wasn't on our list.
But everyone needs a snack to stay happy.
Fine, but just one container and you're sharing with Jake and Tom.
Yes, you're the best.
Okay, vegetables next.
Hold on, what about toilet paper?
Oh, yeah. I forgot that we're out.
We should buy some now, or Tom will yell like the world is ending.
And here I thought you were the dramatic one.
Picnic with housemates.
This was such a good idea. When was the last time we did something outdoors?
Probably never. We're usually too busy with work or stuck with Lily's daily drama.
And yet, you'd all be bored without me.
You're welcome.
Look, there are ducks over there.
Aw, the baby ducks. They're so cute.
Can we feed them some bread?
Actually, bread isn't good for ducks. It makes them sick.
Ah, everything fun is either dangerous or not allowed.
>> [laughter] >> You guys are like a sitcom family. I love it.
Well, you're part of the family now. No escaping us.
I wouldn't want to escape. This is exactly what I needed.
Nighttime routine.
Ooh, doing your skin care routine.
Yeah, just the basics. Face wash, toner, and cream. You do it, too?
Mhm. Mine has like one, 2, 3, 9 steps.
Nine? How long does that take you?
About 40 minutes.
But look at my skin. It's glowing.
It really is. Maybe I should add more steps to my routine.
You should. Let me make you a list.
Oh, no need. I'll just stick with my simple stuff for now.
So, what do you do before bed besides skin care? Like, do you read or anything?
Usually, I write in my journal for a few minutes or just read a book.
That sounds peaceful. I just scroll my phone till I pass out.
That's why you can't sleep, Lily. The blue light keeps your brain awake.
Maybe, but Tik Tok keeps my soul happy.
Until 1:00 a.m.?
Hey, glowing skin and a happy soul, I call that balance.
Tell you what, tomorrow night, no Tik Tok and two chapters of a book before bed. Deal?
Only if you try my nine-step skin care first.
Mhm, deal.

>> Woo!
English speaking course.
The earthquake seems very strong.
Huh? What's happening? Oh, no.
Hey, watch out.
Hey!
Ouch.
Are you okay?
Oh, yes, I think so, but what just happened?
I think it's an earthquake.
I think so, but why did you push me?
A flower pot fell from the balcony. It almost fell on you.
Oh, that was really close. Are you okay?
I'm okay, but my hand hurts a little. I fell down when I pushed you.
I'm so sorry, and thank you so much for saving me.
It's not your fault.
Oh, no. Your knee is bleeding.
Don't worry. It's just a scratch cut.
Anyway, thank you so much. You really saved me. If it wasn't for you, my head could have been injured.
Are you two all right?
The earthquake seems very strong. I saw the flower pot fall.
That was very dangerous.
We're okay, but we both have some scratches.
I feel a bit dizzy now.
Don't worry. I will get help. There's a hospital nearby.
Thank you. We really appreciate it.
The ambulance will arrive soon. They will check your injuries.
I'm scared, but I feel better knowing help is coming.
Thank you again for your help.
I just reacted without thinking. I'm really glad you're okay.
Everything will be fine, you guys. Try to stay calm and take deep breaths.
Are you lost?
La la la. You are my sunshine.
Huh?
Oh.
Mom, where are you?
Mom!
Mom!
No.
Oh, I think I'm lost.
Hello, little one. Why are you crying?
Are you lost?
Um I can't see my mom.
Calm down. Calm down.
I will help you find your mom. Don't cry.
Really? Thank you.
Of course. Can you tell me where you last saw your mom?
Well, I was playing near the playground.
I saw a beautiful butterfly and ran after it.
When I looked back, I couldn't see my mom anywhere.
I see. That happens sometimes. Now, take a deep breath.
Tell me, what is your name and how old are you?
My name is Lucy. I'm 12 years old.
Good. Do you know your mom's phone number?
Yes, she told me to remember it.
1 1 2 3 3 4 5 5 6 Okay, I'll call her now.
Hello, is this Lucy's mom?
Yes, you see my daughter? Is she okay?
Your daughter is safe. She's with me at the park near the fountain.
Oh, thank you so much. I'm coming now.
Please stay with her.
Don't worry. I will.
Your mom will be here soon.
Thank you.
Lucy.
Mom, I'm here.
Are you okay?
I was scared, but she helped me.
I'm sorry, Mom. I won't run away again.
Oh, my dear. I'm so sorry, too. I didn't notice you walked away.
I wasn't paying attention. I'm so glad you're okay.
Thank you so much for taking care of my daughter.
You're welcome. Kids are curious and easily distracted.
You're right. I will keep a closer eye on her from now on.
Next time, stay close to your mom, okay?
Yes, I will. I promise.
Thank you again for your help.
No problem at all. I'm glad she's safe.
Helping someone in need.
Oh, no.
It's stuck.
Excuse me, sir. Are you [music] okay?
It looks like you need some help.
Yes, please. One of my wheels got stuck on that stone.
Don't worry. I'm here to help.
Are you hurt?
No, I'm not hurt. I'm just stuck.
Okay, please stay still for a moment.
Thank you so much. I really appreciate it.
Here we go. This little stone was the problem.
What a naughty one.
That's right. Are you comfortable now?
Great, it's moving now.
Your wheelchair is too cool.
Yeah, but sometimes it has trouble with rocks.
Thank you for your help.
You're welcome. This road isn't very smooth.
That's true. I didn't see the stone on the road.
I understand. I used to jog here every morning.
I once tripped over a stone like this and almost fell.
That sounds dangerous.
That can happen to anyone.
Do you want me to help you get to a safer place?
Thank you, Ryan, but I'm okay now. I can use my wheelchair.
Oh, got it.
You're very kind.
I'm glad I could help. Take care and stay safe.
Thanks again. Have a good day. Bye.
Bye.
Can you give me directions?
>> [music]
[music] >> Excuse me, sorry to bother you.
Huh?
I'm a tourist here and I think I'm a bit lost.
No problem at all. Where are you trying to go?
I'm looking for the Old Town Market.
I wanted to walk there, but the streets all look the same to me.
Can you give me directions?
Yeah, it's easy to get lost around here.
Are you walking or using public transport?
I'm walking. I enjoy exploring the city on foot.
That's a great way to see the city.
You're pretty close.
Really? That's good to hear.
First, go straight down this street for about 5 minutes.
You'll pass a bakery on your left and a small bookstore.
Okay, straight ahead, bakery then bookstore.
After the bookstore, turn right at the traffic light. Keep walking until you see a large square.
A large square, got it.
It's right across the square next to the clock tower.
That sounds much clearer than my map.
Thank you so much for your help.
You're very welcome. Enjoy the market and have a great time in our city.
I will. Everyone here is so kind. Thanks again.
Bye.
Bye.
Crossing the street on a red light.
Wait a second, sweetie. I need to check my phone.
Okay, auntie.
Wait here. Don't move. It's red light now.
Auntie, can we go now?
One moment.
I'm going.
No, wait.
Hey, watch out.
>> [screaming] >> Oh, no.
Are you okay? Alan, are you hurt?
Auntie, I'm scared.
What are you doing? The light is red. I almost hit the child.
I'm so sorry. This is my fault.
I was looking at my phone and didn't notice him.
Please be careful next time. Crossing the street on a red light is very dangerous.
And you see, my car was damaged.
I understand. I'm really sorry.
Is the child all right?
He is fine now. Thanks for your concern.
I am very sorry about this accident and I want to take full responsibility.
May I have your contact information for the compensation?
Of course, that's fair.
Auntie, are we in trouble?
No, sweetie. Everything is okay now.
Please be careful next time, especially with children.
I will.
From now on, I will put my phone away and keep my eye on you.
Dad, are you sure this pasta won't burn?
It looks kind of bubbly.
That's just the water boiling. Cook it more, Alex.
I hope Mom likes it. She always says your cooking is different.
Hey, different is good.
And with your help, it'll be perfect.
Should we light some candles, too?
It'll look special.
Great idea. Let's make this place nice for her.
>> [music]
>> Oh, wow. Something smells so good.
What's going on here?
Welcome home, honey.
Surprise. We made dinner tonight.
Hi, Mom. Guess what? Dad let me be the chef's helper. I boiled the pasta.
You two really did this for me?
That's so lovely. This is the sweetest thing ever.
You worked a lot. We think you should go home and rest.
That's right, Mommy. Daddy and I cooked this meal for you.
Thank you, both of you.
Sit down, take a breath. Dinner's ready.
Mom, you have to try the pasta first.
I cooked the pasta myself.
You guys are the best. This makes me happy.
>> [music]
>> Okay, let's cook dinner together.
Who does what?
I will cook the chicken first.
Can I make the salad?
I like cutting vegetables and mixing them.
Yes, but be careful with the knife, sweetie. I'll help you cut the onion.
That's great, Dad. I don't like cutting onions.
Onions make me cry.
Smells good.
How long for the chicken, honey?
About 40 minutes.
Can I taste the soup now?
Sure. Just a little. Be careful, it's hot.
Mmm, it's delicious.
Great. Dinner is ready.
Let's set the table.
Hi, Jake. I brought you some flowers.
Hi, Emily. Thank you. They are beautiful.
You're welcome.
I also brought a small chocolate.
Wow, that's great. I feel a little better already.
Feeling better today?
A little tired, but better than yesterday.
Did you sleep well last night?
Not really. My head still hurts a bit.
Oh, I'm sorry. Do you want some water?
Yes, please. Thank you.
Here you go. Drink slowly.
Thanks, Emily. That helps.
Emily, did anything fun happen at school today?
We had a football match today. Don't you remember?
Oh, right.
We played with James's class.
Really? Who won?
Of course, our class.
Wow, I wish I was there.
Uh I have some good news for you.
What is it?
The school will get a new coach for our football team.
Really? I really can't wait to be back on the team.
Sure. I think the team really misses you.
I hope so. I want to play again soon.
Don't worry. You'll be back soon and we'll all have fun together.
Hi, Mr. Johnson. Do you have a moment?
Sure, come in, Sarah. What's up?
I want to ask if I can take a few days off next week.
I have some family matters to take care of.
I see.
How many days are you thinking about?
Three days.
From Monday to Wednesday.
All right. Is it something important?
Yes, my cousin is getting married and my parents need my help with the preparations.
Oh, congratulations to your cousin.
Thank you. I will finish my work before I go.
So nothing will be late.
That's good to hear.
Make sure to update the team about your work, okay?
Of course, I'll send an email and let everyone know what I'm working on.
Okay, Sarah. You can have 3 days off.
Enjoy the wedding and take care of your family.
Thank you very much, Mr. Johnson. I'm very happy.
No problem. Have a great time.
And don't worry about work while you're away.
I will. Thanks again.
Hello.
Hello. This is Lisa from NextWave Creative.
May I speak with Mr. Carter, please?
This is Carter speaking. How can I help you?
Mr. Carter, I'm calling to confirm our meeting next Tuesday at 2:00 p.m.
Is that still okay for you?
Yes, Tuesday at 2:00 p.m. works for me.
Thank you for calling.
Great. We will meet at your office, right?
Yes, that's correct. I'll see you there.
Perfect. And just to double-check, will anyone else from your team join the meeting?
Yes, my assistant and one of our project managers will join.
Got it. Thank you for letting me know.
We'll bring all the necessary documents and a presentation for for project.
Sounds good. I'm looking forward to it.
Me too. If anything changes, please let me know.
Then, see you Tuesday at 2:00.
Will do. Thanks for the call, Lisa.
You're welcome, Mr. Carter. Have a great day.
You too. Goodbye.
Hey Tom, are you ready for the science test tomorrow?
Hi Lily. Not really.
I don't understand chapter three about plants.
Me too. Do you want to study together?
Yes, where can we meet?
How about the library? It is quiet there.
Okay, what time?
Can we meet at 4:00 p.m.?
Sure, I will bring my notes and textbook.
I will bring my flash cards with words and pictures.
Good. I have many questions about chapter three.
Me, too. Let's read slowly and write the steps.
Okay.
Do you have a book to help with this chapter?
Yes, I have one extra book.
It is easy to understand.
Really? Can I see it tomorrow?
Yes, we can also draw pictures. It helps me remember.
Good idea.
I like drawing pictures. It is easy to remember.
And we can write key words on flash cards.
Then we can test each other.
Yes, that will help a lot.
I agree. See you tomorrow at 4:00 in the library.
See you. Don't forget your textbook.
>> Wow, that was a good movie.
Yes, I really liked it.
The story was very interesting.
Me, too.
And the main actor was amazing.
He acted really well.
I agree. I also liked the music. It made some scenes more exciting.
Oh, yes. Some scary parts.
The music made them more scary.
I was a little scared, too.
That scene with the monster.
Wow.
I know. My heart was beating fast.
Which part did you like the most?
I liked the ending a lot. It was surprising.
Really? I liked the middle part more.
The big fight was my favorite.
Yes, that was very exciting, too.
The film was so scary, but the funny parts were great.
I laughed a lot at the cat scene.
Me, too. That cat is so silly.
I want to see the next movie.
Same here. We should go together again.
Yes, that would be fun.
Hi, Max. Anna said you wanted to see me.
Yes, Sophie. It's for you.
I I think you will like it.
Oh, thank you. What is it?
It's chocolate. I saw this and and thought of you.
Chocolate? You didn't have to.
I wanted to.
I hope it makes you happy.
Oh, that's really sweet.
I'm glad you like it. Do you want to try some now?
Sure, let's share it.
I got your favorite, milk chocolate with almonds.
You remembered. That's so sweet.
By the way, there's a new movie out this weekend.
Want to go see it together?
Really? That sounds fun. I'd love to go with you.
Great. I think it'll be fun, and we can share some more chocolate there, too.
Perfect.
